text: The product shall run as a stand-alone application.
The product shall use the Qt graphical library.
The hardware shall provide a graphical display of at least 800x600 resolution.
The product shall interface with the user via a keyboard and a mouse.
The system shall allow the user to undo and redo the last thousand actions.
The product shall count and display the time that the user uses to play.
The product shall count and display the number of actions performed by the user.
The product shall record the player score consisting of time and action count associated with a user name.
The product shall display a window containing all recorded player scores.
The main window shall include a board and a menu bar.
The board height shall be 5x and its width shall be 4x where x is between 50 and 100 pixels.
The system shall separate blocks on the board by a margin of 0.1x.
The board shall contain four square blocks with a side of x.
The board shall contain four rectangular blocks with a height of 2x and a width of x.
The board shall contain one block with a height of x and a width of 2x.
The board shall contain one square block with a side of 2x.
The board color shall be black and the block colors shall be yellow.
The system shall change the game state to Block deplacement when a block is left-clicked down.
The system shall change the game state to Block selection when a selected block is left-clicked up.
The selected block shall follow mouse movement without overlapping other blocks or exiting the game zone.
The selected block shall maintain a distance of at least 0.05x from other blocks during movement.
The system shall display the block positions as they were before the last movement when the Undo action is selected.
The system shall disable the Undo action if no previous movement exists.
The system shall display the block positions as they were before a canceled movement when the Redo action is selected.
The system shall disable the Redo action if the previous action was not an Undo action.
The system shall freeze all player statistics when the great square block is moved to the bottom of the board.
The system shall display the Finish Window with Statistics if the current player movement count is lower than the record in the statistic file.
The system shall display the Simple Finish Window if the current movement count is not lower than the record in the statistic file.
The Finish Window with Statistics shall contain the text "You win! Enter your name:" and an edit box for 20 characters.
The system shall record player statistics in the statistic file when the OK button is clicked in the Finish Window with Statistics.
The system shall display an error message if the statistic file cannot be updated due to permissions or disk space.
The system shall maintain a maximum of 10 player statistics in the game statistic file.
The system shall erase the record with the greatest number of movements if a new record is added when 10 statistics already exist.
The system shall erase all data in the statistic file when the Erase menu option is selected.
The Statistic Window shall display a listbox of 10 lines containing player name, movement count, and time.
The system shall open a dialog box to allow the user to select and open a previously saved game file.
The system shall redraw the board and set player statistics according to the data in the opened file.
The system shall save the current block positions, the last 10000 previous positions, action count, and time into the current file.
The system shall prompt the user to save the game via a dialog box if the user selects Exit and the game is not saved.
The software shall be portable to the Windows operating system.
